lede: A look at September 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Coco Yoshizawa and Katie Lowes, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Anura Kumara Dissanayake
events.desc: Anura Kumara Dissanayake is elected as the 9th President Of Sri Lanka.

events.title: Military exercise
events.desc: During a military exercise, a Turkish Air Force Northrop F-5 crashes in Babaeski as a result of pilot error, killing one crew member and also 65 soldiers on the ground.

events.title: Sara Jane Moore
events.desc: Sara Jane Moore tries to assassinate U.S. President Gerald Ford, but is foiled by the Secret Service.

events.title: Four Level Interchange
events.desc: The Four Level Interchange, first stack interchange in the world opened in Los Angeles.

events.title: Gail Halvorsen
events.desc: Gail Halvorsen officially starts parachuting candy to children as part of the Berlin Airlift.

events.title: Gresford disaster
events.desc: The Gresford disaster in Wales kills 266 miners and rescuers.

events.title: Lindal railway incident
events.desc: A locomotive shunting falls into a hole in the ground, leading to the burial of the locomotive.

events.title: United States Postmaster General
events.desc: The office of United States Postmaster General is established.

events.title: Battle of Rymnik
events.desc: Battle of Rymnik: Alexander Suvorov's Russian and allied army defeats superior Ottoman Empire forces.

events.title: Eighty Years' War
events.desc: Eighty Years' War: A Spanish force led by the Marquis del Vasto successfully fights its way past a joint English/Dutch ambush in the Battle of Zutphen.

births.name: T. S. Sinnathuray
births.line: Judge of the High Court of Singapore (died 2016)

births.name: Christabel Pankhurst
births.line: English activist, co-founded the Women's Social and Political Union (died 1958)

deaths.text: Rais Amrohvi, Pakistani psychoanalyst, scholar, and poet
Solomon L. Spink, American lawyer and politician

observances.text: Christian feast days: Ignatius of Santhià (Lorenzo Maurizio Belvisotti)
